title: Reconfigure a Replica Set During a Regional Outage

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
Your cluster must be experiencing a total outage of nodes in one or more
regions with at least one electable node remaining in another region.
To learn how to simulate a regional outage for your multi-region cluster,
see [Simulate Regional Outage](test-outage).

For assistance with other causes of node outages, [MongoDB Support](request-support).

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
To restore your Atlas multi-region cluster from an outage, you
must manually reconfigure your replica set:

sections.heading: Step 2: Select the cluster you want to recover from the drop-down menu.
Step 3: Click .
sections.body:
Under **Electable nodes for high availability**, you
can add new nodes to:

- An existing region by editing the number of nodes in the **Nodes** column

- A new region by clicking **+ Add a provider/region**

> **Tip:** We strongly advise against adding nodes to regions that are
> affected by the outage.

sections.heading: Step 4: Click .
sections.body:
Review your changes to the cluster configuration and pricing.

If you're adding an electable node to a new AWS (Amazon Web Services) region that is
not affected by the outage, you can toggle **Enable
Faster Cross-Region Atlas Initial Syncs for AWS Clusters** to
**ON** to speed up the cross-region data copy between
AWS (Amazon Web Services) nodes for an additional cost. This option only appears if
you're adding a node to an AWS (Amazon Web Services) region without the primary node,
and if you have not previously enabled this option in the
cluster settings. To learn more about this option, see
[Enable Faster Cross-Region Initial Sync for AWS Clusters](enable_time_based_initial_sync_aws).
